evals.prompt: Our project was supposed to be 50% done ($500k PV). We've spent $600k (AC) but we are only 40% done (EV = $400k). How are we doing?
evals.expected_output: Should calculate CPI (400/600 = 0.67). Should calculate SPI (400/500 = 0.80). Should conclude the project is over budget and behind schedule. Should provide a Project Performance Dashboard.
assertions: Concludes project is over budget and behind schedule
Mentions the EAC (Estimate at Completion) forecast
Output follows Project Performance Dashboard structure

evals.prompt: Project: BAC 50M, 40% complete (EV 20M), AC 26M, PV 24M. Compute CPI, SPI, and a defensible EAC range.
evals.expected_output: CPI = 0.769, SPI = 0.833. EAC range: optimistic AC+(BAC-EV)=56M; CPI-based 50/0.769=65M; combined CPI x SPI ~ 72.8M. TCPI to budget = 30/24 = 1.25 - recovery unrealistic; report the range with the CPI-based as the likely floor at this completion stage.
assertions: Computes TCPI of 1.25 and calls recovery unrealistic
Notes CPI stability research making 65M a floor, not a ceiling

evals.prompt: Our SPI is 0.98 so the PM says schedule is fine, but the critical-path milestone slipped 2 months. Reconcile.
evals.expected_output: SPI aggregates all work - off-critical-path tasks running ahead mask critical-path slippage; SPI($) also drifts to 1.0 late in projects. Use earned schedule SPI(t) and critical-path analysis; the milestone slip is the real signal.
assertions: Explains SPI masking via non-critical work
Mentions SPI($) converging to 1.0 as completion approaches
Recommends earned schedule and critical-path tracking
Prioritizes the milestone slip over the index

evals.prompt: Assess the project in the attached status file (evals/files/project_status.csv): compute CV, SV, CPI, SPI, EAC (CPI method), and TCPI, and state whether the original budget is still credible.
assertions: TCPI approx 1.05 - concludes hitting BAC requires above-baseline efficiency
